v.CNV: “你们是耶和华你们的　神的儿女，不可为了死人割伤自己的身体，也不可使你们的额上光秃；
v.ESV: “You are the sons of the Lord your God. You shall not cut yourselves or make any baldness on your foreheads for the dead.
v.GAE: 너희는 너희 하나님 여호와의 자녀이니 죽은 자를 위하여 자기 몸을 베지 말며 눈썹 사이 이마 위의 털을 밀지 말라
v.KJV: Ye are the children of the LORD your God: ye shall not cut yourselves, nor make any baldness between your eyes for the dead.
v.NASB: 'You are the sons of the LORD your God; you shall not cut yourselves nor shave your forehead for the sake of the dead.
v.NIV: You are the children of the Lord your God. Do not cut yourselves or shave the front of your heads for the dead,
v.NRSV: You are children of the LORD your God. You must not lacerate yourselves or shave your forelocks for the dead.
v.SAENEW: "당신들은 주 당신들의 하나님의 자녀이니, 죽은 사람을 애도할 때에 몸에 상처를 내거나 앞머리를 밀어서는 안 됩니다.
v.WLB: “너희는 너희 하나님 여호와의 자손이니 죽은 사람을 위해 너희 몸을 상하게 하거나 눈썹 사이 이마를 밀지 말라.

v.CNV: 因为你是属于耶和华你的　神的圣洁子民；耶和华从地上的万民中拣选了你们，特作他自己的子民。
v.ESV: For you are a people holy to the Lord your God, and the Lord has chosen you to be a people for his treasured possession, out of all the peoples who are on the face of the earth.
v.GAE: 너는 네 하나님 여호와의 성민이라 여호와께서 지상 만민 중에서 너를 택하여 자기 기업의 백성으로 삼으셨느니라
v.KJV: for thou art an holy people unto the LORD thy God, and the LORD hath chosen thee to be a peculiar people unto himself, above all the nations that are upon the earth.
v.NASB: 'For you are a holy people to the LORD your God, and the LORD has chosen you to be a people for His own possession out of all the peoples who are on the face of the earth.
v.NIV: for you are a people holy to the Lord your God. Out of all the peoples on the face of the earth, the Lord has chosen you to be his treasured possession.
v.NRSV: For you are a people holy to the LORD your God; it is you the LORD has chosen out of all the peoples on earth to be his people, his treasured possession.
v.SAENEW: 당신들은 주 당신들의 하나님의 거룩한 백성입니다. 주님께서 땅 위에 있는 많은 백성 가운데서 당신들을 선택하여, 자기의 귀중한 백성으로 삼으셨습니다."
v.WLB: 이는 너희가 너희 하나님 여호와께 거룩한 백성이기 때문이다. 지상의 모든 민족들 가운데 여호와께서 너희를 선택해 그분의 소중한 소유물이 되게 하셨다.

v.CNV: “可厌恶之物，你都不可吃。
v.ESV: “You shall not eat any abomination.
v.GAE: 너는 가증한 것은 무엇이든지 먹지 말라
v.KJV: Thou shalt not eat any abominable thing.
v.NASB: 'You shall not eat any detestable thing.
v.NIV: Do not eat any detestable thing.
v.NRSV: You shall not eat any abhorrent thing.
v.SAENEW: "당신들은 주님께서 부정하다고 하신 것은 어떤 것도 먹어서는 안 됩니다.
v.WLB: 가증스러운 것은 어떤 것이든 먹지 말라.

v.CNV: 你们可以吃的牲畜是以下这些：牛、绵羊、山羊、
v.ESV: These are the animals you may eat: the ox, the sheep, the goat,
v.GAE: 너희가 먹을 만한 짐승은 이러하니 곧 소와 양과 염소와
v.KJV: These are the beasts which ye shall eat: the ox, the sheep, and the goat,
v.NASB: 'These are the animals which you may eat: the ox, the sheep, the goat,
v.NIV: These are the animals you may eat: the ox, the sheep, the goat,
v.NRSV: These are the animals you may eat: the ox, the sheep, the goat,
v.SAENEW: 당신들이 먹을 수 있는 짐승은, 소와 양과 염소와
v.WLB: 너희가 먹어도 되는 동물들은 소, 양, 염소,

v.CNV: 鹿、羚羊、赤鹿、野山羊、麋鹿、野羊、野鹿。
v.ESV: the deer, the gazelle, the roebuck, the wild goat, the ibex, the antelope, and the mountain sheep.
v.GAE: 사슴과 노루와 불그스름한 사슴과 산 염소와 볼기가 흰 노루와 뿔이 긴 사슴과 산양들이라
v.KJV: The hart, and the roebuck, and the fallow deer, and the wild goat, and the pygarg, and the wild ox, and the chamois.
v.NASB: the deer, the gazelle, the roebuck, the wild goat, the ibex, the antelope and the mountain sheep.
v.NIV: the deer, the gazelle, the roe deer, the wild goat, the ibex, the antelope and the mountain sheep.
v.NRSV: the deer, the gazelle, the roebuck, the wild goat, the ibex, the antelope, and the mountain-sheep.
v.SAENEW: 사슴과 노루와 꽃사슴과 들염소와 산염소와 들양과 산양과 같이
v.WLB: 사슴, 노루, 불그스름한 사슴, 산염소, 야생염소, 영양, 산양 등이다.

v.CNV: 在走兽中，分蹄成两蹄趾，又反刍的走兽，你们都可以吃。
v.ESV: Every animal that parts the hoof and has the hoof cloven in two and chews the cud, among the animals, you may eat.
v.GAE: 짐승 중에 굽이 갈라져 쪽발도 되고 새김질도 하는 모든 것은 너희가 먹을 것이니라
v.KJV: And every beast that parteth the hoof, and cleaveth the cleft into two claws, and cheweth the cud among the beasts, that ye shall eat.
v.NASB: 'Any animal that divides the hoof and has the hoof split in two and chews the cud, among the animals, that you may eat.
v.NIV: You may eat any animal that has a divided hoof and that chews the cud.
v.NRSV: Any animal that divides the hoof and has the hoof cleft in two, and chews the cud, among the animals, you may eat.
v.SAENEW: 굽이 두 쪽으로 갈라진 쪽발이면서, 새김질도 하는 모든 짐승입니다. 이것들은 당신들이 먹을 수 있습니다.
v.WLB: 굽이 둘로 갈라지고 되새김질하는 동물은 너희가 먹어도 되지만

v.CNV: 但是那些反刍或分蹄的走兽中，你们不可吃的有：骆驼、兔子和石獾，因为它们反刍却不分蹄，就对你们不洁净。
v.ESV: Yet of those that chew the cud or have the hoof cloven you shall not eat these: the camel, the hare, and the rock badger, because they chew the cud but do not part the hoof, are unclean for you.
v.GAE: 다만 새김질을 하거나 굽이 갈라진 짐승 중에도 너희가 먹지 못할 것은 이것이니 곧 낙타와 토끼와 사반, 그것들은 새김질은 하나 굽이 갈라지지 아니하였으니 너희에게 부정하고
v.KJV: Nevertheless these ye shall not eat of them that chew the cud, or of them that divide the cloven hoof; as the camel, and the hare, and the coney: for they chew the cud, but divide not the hoof; therefore they are unclean unto you.
v.NASB: 'Nevertheless, you are not to eat of these among those which chew the cud, or among those that divide the hoof in two: the camel and the rabbit and the shaphan, for though they chew the cud, they do not divide the hoof; they are unclean for you.
v.NIV: However, of those that chew the cud or that have a divided hoof you may not eat the camel, the rabbit or the hyrax. Although they chew the cud, they do not have a divided hoof; they are ceremonially unclean for you.
v.NRSV: Yet of those that chew the cud or have the hoof cleft you shall not eat these: the camel, the hare, and the rock badger, because they chew the cud but do not divide the hoof; they are unclean for you.
v.SAENEW: 새김질을 하거나 굽이 두 쪽으로 갈라졌더라도, 다음과 같은 것들은 당신들이 먹지 못합니다. 낙타와 토끼와 오소리와 같은 짐승은, 새김질은 하지만 굽이 갈라지지 않았으므로 당신들에게 부정한 것입니다.
v.WLB: 되새김질하는 것이나 굽이 갈라진 것 가운데 너희가 먹으면 안 되는 것은 낙타, 토끼, 오소리다. 그것들은 되새김질을 하지만 굽이 갈라지지 않았으니 너희에게는 부정하다.

v.CNV: 至于猪，因为它分蹄却不反刍，就对你们不洁净。它们的肉，你们不可吃；它们的尸体，你们不可触摸。
v.ESV: And the pig, because it parts the hoof but does not chew the cud, is unclean for you. Their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall not touch.
v.GAE: 돼지는 굽은 갈라졌으나 새김질을 못하므로 너희에게 부정하니 너희는 이런 것의 고기를 먹지 말 것이며 그 사체도 만지지 말 것이니라
v.KJV: And the swine, because it divideth the hoof, yet cheweth not the cud, it is unclean unto you: ye shall not eat of their flesh, nor touch their dead carcase.
v.NASB: 'The pig, because it divides the hoof but does not chew the cud, it is unclean for you. You shall not eat any of their flesh nor touch their carcasses.
v.NIV: The pig is also unclean; although it has a divided hoof, it does not chew the cud. You are not to eat their meat or touch their carcasses.
v.NRSV: And the pig, because it divides the hoof but does not chew the cud, is unclean for you. You shall not eat their meat, and you shall not touch their carcasses.
v.SAENEW: 돼지는 굽은 갈라졌지만 새김질을 하지 않으므로 당신들에게 부정한 것입니다. 당신들은 이런 짐승의 고기를 먹어서는 안 되고, 그것들의 주검도 만져서는 안 됩니다.
v.WLB: 돼지도 부정하니 이는 굽이 갈라져 있지만 되새김질을 하지 않기 때문이다. 너희는 그 고기를 먹거나 죽은 것을 만지지 말라.

v.CNV: “在水中，你们可以吃的，有以下这些：有鳍有鳞的，你们都可以吃。
v.ESV: “Of all that are in the waters you may eat these: whatever has fins and scales you may eat.
v.GAE: 물에 있는 모든 것 중에서 이런 것은 너희가 먹을 것이니 지느러미와 비늘 있는 모든 것은 너희가 먹을 것이요
v.KJV: These ye shall eat of all that are in the waters: all that have fins and scales shall ye eat:
v.NASB: 'These you may eat of all that are in water: anything that has fins and scales you may eat,
v.NIV: Of all the creatures living in the water, you may eat any that has fins and scales.
v.NRSV: Of all that live in water you may eat these: whatever has fins and scales you may eat.
v.SAENEW: 물에서 사는 모든 것 가운데서 지느러미가 있고 비늘이 있는 물고기는, 무엇이든지 당신들이 먹을 수 있습니다.
v.WLB: 물속에서 사는 모든 생물들 가운데서는 지느러미와 비늘이 있는 것은 다 너희가 먹어도 된다.

v.CNV: 有鳍没有鳞的，你们都不可吃；这是对你们不洁净的。
v.ESV: And whatever does not have fins and scales you shall not eat; it is unclean for you.
v.GAE: 지느러미와 비늘이 없는 모든 것은 너희가 먹지 말지니 이는 너희에게 부정함이니라
v.KJV: And whatsoever hath not fins and scales ye may not eat; it is unclean unto you.
v.NASB: but anything that does not have fins and scales you shall not eat; it is unclean for you.
v.NIV: But anything that does not have fins and scales you may not eat; for you it is unclean.
v.NRSV: And whatever does not have fins and scales you shall not eat; it is unclean for you.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 지느러미가 없고 비늘이 없는 것은, 어떤 것이든지 당신들에게 부정한 것이니, 당신들이 먹어서는 안 됩니다.
v.WLB: 그러나 지느러미와 비늘이 없는 것은 먹지 말라. 그것은 너희에게 부정한 것이기 때문이다.

v.CNV: “洁净的鸟类，你们都可以吃。
v.ESV: “You may eat all clean birds.
v.GAE: 정한 새는 모두 너희가 먹으려니와
v.KJV: Of all clean birds ye shall eat.
v.NASB: 'You may eat any clean bird.
v.NIV: You may eat any clean bird.
v.NRSV: You may eat any clean birds.
v.SAENEW: 깨끗한 새는 무엇이든지 당신들이 먹을 수 있습니다.
v.WLB: 정결한 새는 어떤 것이든 먹어도 된다.

v.CNV: 你们不可吃的鸟类有以下这些：鹰、狗头鹰、红头鹰、
v.ESV: But these are the ones that you shall not eat: the eagle, the bearded vulture, the black vulture,
v.GAE: 이런 것은 먹지 못할지니 곧 독수리와 솔개와 물수리와
v.KJV: But these are they of which ye shall not eat: the eagle, and ossifrage, and the ospray,
v.NASB: 'But these are the ones which you shall not eat: the eagle and the vulture and the buzzard,
v.NIV: But these you may not eat: the eagle, the vulture, the black vulture,
v.NRSV: But these are the ones that you shall not eat: the eagle, the vulture, the osprey,
v.SAENEW: 그러나 당신들이 먹지 못하는 새가 있습니다. 그것은, 독수리와 수염수리와 물수리와
v.WLB: 그러나 너희가 먹지 말아야 할 것은 독수리, 솔개, 검은 솔개,

v.CNV: 鸢、隼、黑隼，以及这一类的鸟；
v.ESV: the kite, the falcon of any kind;
v.GAE: 매와 새매와 매의 종류와
v.KJV: And the glede, and the kite, and the vulture after his kind,
v.NASB: and the red kite, the falcon, and the kite in their kinds,
v.NIV: the red kite, the black kite, any kind of falcon,
v.NRSV: the buzzard, the kite of any kind;
v.SAENEW: 매와 붉은소리개와 각종 소리개와
v.WLB: 매, 새매, 그 외의 매 종류,

v.CNV: 乌鸦和这一类的鸟；
v.ESV: every raven of any kind;
v.GAE: 까마귀 종류와
v.KJV: And every raven after his kind,
v.NASB: and every raven in its kind,
v.NIV: any kind of raven,
v.NRSV: every raven of any kind;
v.SAENEW: 각종 모든 까마귀와
v.WLB: 까마귀 종류,

v.CNV: 驼鸟、夜鹰、海鹰、雀鹰，以及这一类的鸟；
v.ESV: the ostrich, the nighthawk, the sea gull, the hawk of any kind;
v.GAE: 타조와 타흐마스와 갈매기와 새매 종류와
v.KJV: And the owl, and the night hawk, and the cuckow, and the hawk after his kind,
v.NASB: and the ostrich, the owl, the sea gull, and the hawk in their kinds,
v.NIV: the horned owl, the screech owl, the gull, any kind of hawk,
v.NRSV: the ostrich, the nighthawk, the sea gull, the hawk of any kind;
v.SAENEW: 타조와 올빼미와 갈매기와 각종 매와
v.WLB: 타조, 헛간 올빼미, 갈매기, 새매 종류,

v.CNV: 小猫头鹰、猫头鹰、角鸱、
v.ESV: the little owl and the short-eared owl, the barn owl
v.GAE: 올빼미와 부엉이와 흰 올빼미와
v.KJV: The little owl, and the great owl, and the swan,
v.NASB: the little owl, the great owl, the white owl,
v.NIV: the little owl, the great owl, the white owl,
v.NRSV: the little owl and the great owl, the water hen
v.SAENEW: 부엉이와 따오기와 백조와
v.WLB: 올빼미, 부엉이, 따오기,

v.CNV: 小枭、秃雕、鱼鹰、
v.ESV: and the tawny owl, the carrion vulture and the cormorant,
v.GAE: 당아와 올응과 노자와
v.KJV: And the pelican, and the gier eagle, and the cormorant,
v.NASB: the pelican, the carrion vulture, the cormorant,
v.NIV: the desert owl, the osprey, the cormorant,
v.NRSV: and the desert owl, the carrion vulture and the cormorant,
v.SAENEW: 펠리컨과 흰물오리와 가마우지와
v.WLB: 거위, 독수리, 물에 잠기는 까마귀,

v.CNV: 鹳、鹭，以及这一类的鸟；戴胜和蝙蝠。
v.ESV: the stork, the heron of any kind; the hoopoe and the bat.
v.GAE: 학과 황새 종류와 대승과 박쥐며
v.KJV: And the stork, and the heron after her kind, and the lapwing, and the bat.
v.NASB: the stork, and the heron in their kinds, and the hoopoe and the bat.
v.NIV: the stork, any kind of heron, the hoopoe and the bat.
v.NRSV: the stork, the heron of any kind; the hoopoe and the bat.
v.SAENEW: 고니와 각종 푸른 해오라기와 오디새와 박쥐입니다.
v.WLB: 학, 황새 종류, 오디새, 박쥐 등이다.

v.CNV: 有翅膀的昆虫，对你们都不洁净，你们都不可吃。
v.ESV: And all winged insects are unclean for you; they shall not be eaten.
v.GAE: 또 날기도 하고 기어다니기도 하는 것은 너희에게 부정하니 너희는 먹지 말 것이나
v.KJV: And every creeping thing that flieth is unclean unto you: they shall not be eaten.
v.NASB: 'And all the teeming life with wings are unclean to you; they shall not be eaten.
v.NIV: All flying insects are unclean to you; do not eat them.
v.NRSV: And all winged insects are unclean for you; they shall not be eaten.
v.SAENEW: 날기도 하고 기어 다니기도 하는 곤충은 당신들에게 부정한 것이니, 먹어서는 안 됩니다.
v.WLB: 모든 떼로 날아다니는 곤충들은 너희에게 부정한 것이니 그것들을 먹지 말라.

v.CNV: 洁净的鸟类，你们都可以吃。
v.ESV: All clean winged things you may eat.
v.GAE: 정한 새는 모두 너희가 먹을지니라
v.KJV: But of all clean fowls ye may eat.
v.NASB: 'You may eat any clean bird.
v.NIV: But any winged creature that is clean you may eat.
v.NRSV: You may eat any clean winged creature.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 깨끗한 날벌레는 당신들이 먹을 수 있습니다.
v.WLB: 그러나 정결한 새는 먹어도 된다.

v.CNV: “自死的动物，你们都不可吃；可以送给你城里的寄居者吃，或是卖给外族人吃，因为你是归耶和华为圣的子民；不可用山羊羔母的奶煮山羊羔。
v.ESV: “You shall not eat anything that has died naturally. You may give it to the sojourner who is within your towns, that he may eat it, or you may sell it to a foreigner. For you are a people holy to the Lord your God. “You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.
v.GAE: 너희는 너희의 하나님 여호와의 성민이라 스스로 죽은 모든 것은 먹지 말 것이나 그것을 성중에 거류하는 객에게 주어 먹게 하거나 이방인에게 파는 것은 가하니라 너는 염소 새끼를 그 어미의 젖에 삶지 말지니라
v.KJV: Ye shall not eat of any thing that dieth of itself: thou shalt give it unto the stranger that is in thy gates, that he may eat it; or thou mayest sell it unto an alien: for thou art an holy people unto the LORD thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother’s milk.
v.NASB: 'You shall not eat anything which dies of itself. You may give it to the alien who is in your town, so that he may eat it, or you may sell it to a foreigner, for you are a holy people to the LORD your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.
v.NIV: Do not eat anything you find already dead. You may give it to the foreigner residing in any of your towns, and they may eat it, or you may sell it to any other foreigner. But you are a people holy to the Lord your God. Do not cook a young goat in its mother’s milk.
v.NRSV: You shall not eat anything that dies of itself; you may give it to aliens residing in your towns for them to eat, or you may sell it to a foreigner. For you are a people holy to the LORD your God. You shall not boil a kid in its mother’s milk.
v.SAENEW: 당신들은 주 당신들의 하나님의 거룩한 백성이므로, 저절로 죽은 것을 먹어서는 안 됩니다. 그런 것은 당신들이 사는 성에 있는 나그네에게 먹으라고 주거나, 이방 사람에게 파십시오.당신들은 새끼 염소를 제 어미의 젖에 삶지 마십시오."
v.WLB: 이미 죽은 것은 무엇이든 먹지 말라. 너희는 그것을 너희 성 가운데 사는 이방 사람에게 주어 먹게 하라. 아니면 이방 사람에게 팔라. 너희는 너희 하나님 여호와께 거룩한 백성이다. 어린 염소를 그 어미의 젖에 삶지 말라.”

v.CNV: “你要把你撒种所产的，就是田地每年所出的，献上十分之一。
v.ESV: “You shall tithe all the yield of your seed that comes from the field year by year.
v.GAE: 너는 마땅히 매 년 토지 소산의 십일조를 드릴 것이며
v.KJV: Thou shalt truly tithe all the increase of thy seed, that the field bringeth forth year by year.
v.NASB: 'You shall surely tithe all the produce from what you sow, which comes out of the field every year.
v.NIV: Be sure to set aside a tenth of all that your fields produce each year.
v.NRSV: Set apart a tithe of all the yield of your seed that is brought in yearly from the field.
v.SAENEW: "당신들은 해마다 밭에서 거둔 소출의 십일조를 드려야 합니다.
v.WLB: “해마다 밭에서 나는 모든 것에서 십일조를 떼는 것을 명심하라.

v.CNV: 又要把你的五谷、新酒和新油的十分之一，以及你牛群羊群中头生的，在耶和华你的　神面前吃，就是在他选择作他名的居所的地方吃，好使你可以学习常常敬畏耶和华你的　神。
v.ESV: And before the Lord your God, in the place that he will choose, to make his name dwell there, you shall eat the tithe of your grain, of your wine, and of your oil, and the firstborn of your herd and flock, that you may learn to fear the Lord your God always.
v.GAE: 네 하나님 여호와 앞 곧 여호와께서 그의 이름을 두시려고 택하신 곳에서 네 곡식과 포도주와 기름의 십일조를 먹으며 또 네 소와 양의 처음 난 것을 먹고 네 하나님 여호와 경외하기를 항상 배울 것이니라
v.KJV: And thou shalt eat before the LORD thy God, in the place which he shall choose to place his name there, the tithe of thy corn, of thy wine, and of thine oil, and the firstlings of thy herds and of thy flocks; that thou mayest learn to fear the LORD thy God always.
v.NASB: 'You shall eat in the presence of the LORD your God, at the place where He chooses to establish His name, the tithe of your grain, your new wine, your oil, and the firstborn of your herd and your flock, so that you may learn to fear the LORD your God always.
v.NIV: Eat the tithe of your grain, new wine and olive oil, and the firstborn of your herds and flocks in the presence of the Lord your God at the place he will choose as a dwelling for his Name, so that you may learn to revere the Lord your God always.
v.NRSV: In the presence of the LORD your God, in the place that he will choose as a dwelling for his name, you shall eat the tithe of your grain, your wine, and your oil, as well as the firstlings of your herd and flock, so that you may learn to fear the LORD your God always.
v.SAENEW: 당신들은, 곡식과 포도주와 기름의 십일조를, 처음 난 소와 양의 새끼와 함께, 주 당신들의 하나님이 자기의 이름을 두려고 택하신 곳으로 가지고 가서, 주님 앞에서 먹어야 합니다. 이렇게 함으로써 당신들은 주 당신들의 하나님을 두려워하는 것을 배우게 됩니다.
v.WLB: 너희 곡식과 기름과 새 포도주의 십일조와 너희 소와 양 가운데 처음 난 것을 너희 하나님 여호와 앞에서 그분께서 자기 이름을 두실 곳으로 선택하시는 그 장소에서 먹도록 하라. 그러면 너희가 너희 하나님 여호와를 항상 경외하는 것을 배울 것이다.

v.CNV: 耶和华你的　神赐福与你的时候，耶和华你的　神选择要立为他名的地方，如果离你太远，那路又太长，使你不能把供物带到那里去；
v.ESV: And if the way is too long for you, so that you are not able to carry the tithe, when the Lord your God blesses you, because the place is too far from you, which the Lord your God chooses, to set his name there,
v.GAE: 그러나 네 하나님 여호와께서 자기의 이름을 두시려고 택하신 곳이 네게서 너무 멀고 행로가 어려워서 네 하나님 여호와께서 그 풍부히 주신 것을 가지고 갈 수 없거든
v.KJV: And if the way be too long for thee, so that thou art not able to carry it; or if the place be too far from thee, which the LORD thy God shall choose to set his name there, when the LORD thy God hath blessed thee:
v.NASB: 'If the distance is so great for you that you are not able to bring the tithe, since the place where the LORD your God chooses to set His name is too far away from you when the LORD your God blesses you,
v.NIV: But if that place is too distant and you have been blessed by the Lord your God and cannot carry your tithe (because the place where the Lord will choose to put his Name is so far away),
v.NRSV: But if, when the LORD your God has blessed you, the distance is so great that you are unable to transport it, because the place where the LORD your God will choose to set his name is too far away from you,
v.SAENEW: 그러나 주 당신들의 하나님이 그의 이름을 두려고 택하신 곳이, 당신들이 있는 곳에서 너무 멀고, 가기가 어려워서, 그것을 가지고 갈 수 없거든,
v.WLB: 그러나 만일 여호와께서 그분의 이름을 두시려고 선택하신 곳이 너무 멀어서 너희가 너희 하나님 여호와께 복을 받았지만 십일조를 가지고 갈 수 없다면

v.CNV: 你就可以换成银子，把银子拿在手里，到耶和华你的　神选择的地方去，
v.ESV: then you shall turn it into money and bind up the money in your hand and go to the place that the Lord your God chooses
v.GAE: 그것을 돈으로 바꾸어 그 돈을 싸 가지고 네 하나님 여호와께서 택하신 곳으로 가서
v.KJV: Then shalt thou turn it into money, and bind up the money in thine hand, and shalt go unto the place which the LORD thy God shall choose:
v.NASB: then you shall exchange it for money, and bind the money in your hand and go to the place which the LORD your God chooses.
v.NIV: then exchange your tithe for silver, and take the silver with you and go to the place the Lord your God will choose.
v.NRSV: then you may turn it into money. With the money secure in hand, go to the place that the LORD your God will choose;
v.SAENEW: 당신들은 그것을 돈으로 바꿔서, 그 돈을 가지고 주 당신들의 하나님이 택하신 곳으로 가서,
v.WLB: 너희 십일조를 은으로 바꾸어 가지고 너희 하나님 여호와께서 선택하신 그곳으로 가라.

v.CNV: 你可以用这银子随意买牛羊、清酒和烈酒；你心想要的，都可以买；在那里，你和你的家人，都要在耶和华你的　神面前吃喝欢乐。
v.ESV: and spend the money for whatever you desire—oxen or sheep or wine or strong drink, whatever your appetite craves. And you shall eat there before the Lord your God and rejoice, you and your household.
v.GAE: 네 마음에 원하는 모든 것을 그 돈으로 사되 소나 양이나 포도주나 독주 등 네 마음에 원하는 모든 것을 구하고 거기 네 하나님 여호와 앞에서 너와 네 권속이 함께 먹고 즐거워할 것이며
v.KJV: And thou shalt bestow that money for whatsoever thy soul lusteth after, for oxen, or for sheep, or for wine, or for strong drink, or for whatsoever thy soul desireth: and thou shalt eat there before the LORD thy God, and thou shalt rejoice, thou, and thine household,
v.NASB: 'You may spend the money for whatever your heart desires: for oxen, or sheep, or wine, or strong drink, or whatever your heart desires; and there you shall eat in the presence of the LORD your God and rejoice, you and your household.
v.NIV: Use the silver to buy whatever you like: cattle, sheep, wine or other fermented drink, or anything you wish. Then you and your household shall eat there in the presence of the Lord your God and rejoice.
v.NRSV: spend the money for whatever you wish — oxen, sheep, wine, strong drink, or whatever you desire. And you shall eat there in the presence of the LORD your God, you and your household rejoicing together.
v.SAENEW: 그 돈으로 마음에 드는 것을 사십시오. 소든지 양이든지 포도주든지 독한 술이든지, 어떤 것이든지 먹고 싶은 것을 사서, 주 당신들의 하나님 앞에서 당신들과 당신들의 온 가족이 함께 먹으면서 즐거워하십시오.
v.WLB: 그 은을 가지고 너희가 원하는 것을 사되 소, 양, 포도주, 다른 종류의 술 등 너희가 원하는 어떤 것이든 사라. 거기서 너희와 너희 가족이 너희 하나님 여호와 앞에서 먹고 즐거워하라.

v.CNV: 住在你城里的利未人，你不可丢弃他，因为他在你们中间无分无业。
v.ESV: And you shall not neglect the Levite who is within your towns, for he has no portion or inheritance with you.
v.GAE: 네 성읍에 거주하는 레위인은 너희 중에 분깃이나 기업이 없는 자이니 또한 저버리지 말지니라
v.KJV: And the Levite that is within thy gates; thou shalt not forsake him; for he hath no part nor inheritance with thee.
v.NASB: 'Also you shall not neglect the Levite who is in your town, for he has no portion or inheritance among you.
v.NIV: And do not neglect the Levites living in your towns, for they have no allotment or inheritance of their own.
v.NRSV: As for the Levites resident in your towns, do not neglect them, because they have no allotment or inheritance with you.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 성 안에서 당신들과 함께 사는 레위 사람은, 유산도 없고 차지할 몫도 없는 사람들이니, 그들을 저버리지 않도록 하십시오.
v.WLB: 그리고 너희 성에 사는 레위 사람들을 무시하지 말라. 그들은 그들 몫이나 기업이 없기 때문이다.

v.CNV: “每三年之末，你要把那一年的全部出产的十分之一取出来，积存在你的城里；
v.ESV: “At the end of every three years you shall bring out all the tithe of your produce in the same year and lay it up within your towns.
v.GAE: 매 삼 년 끝에 그 해 소산의 십분의 일을 다 내어 네 성읍에 저축하여
v.KJV: At the end of three years thou shalt bring forth all the tithe of thine increase the same year, and shalt lay it up within thy gates:
v.NASB: 'At the end of every third year you shall bring out all the tithe of your produce in that year, and shall deposit it in your town.
v.NIV: At the end of every three years, bring all the tithes of that year’s produce and store it in your towns,
v.NRSV: Every third year you shall bring out the full tithe of your produce for that year, and store it within your towns;
v.SAENEW: 당신들은 매 삼 년 끝에 그 해에 난 소출의 십일조를 다 모아서 성 안에 저장하여 두었다가,
v.WLB: 3년마다 그 해에 얻은 것의 모든 십일조를 가져다가 너희 성안에 저장하라.

v.CNV: 这样，利未人（因为他在你们中间无分无业），以及在你城里的寄居者和孤儿寡妇，就可以来，吃得饱足；好叫耶和华你的　神，在你手所作的一切事上，赐福给你。”
v.ESV: And the Levite, because he has no portion or inheritance with you, and the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow, who are within your towns, shall come and eat and be filled, that the Lord your God may bless you in all the work of your hands that you do.
v.GAE: 너희 중에 분깃이나 기업이 없는 레위인과 네 성중에 거류하는 객과 및 고아와 과부들이 와서 먹고 배부르게 하라 그리하면 네 하나님 여호와께서 네 손으로 하는 범사에 네게 복을 주시리라
v.KJV: And the Levite, (because he hath no part nor inheritance with thee,) and the stranger, and the fatherless, and the widow, which are within thy gates, shall come, and shall eat and be satisfied; that the LORD thy God may bless thee in all the work of thine hand which thou doest.
v.NASB: 'The Levite, because he has no portion or inheritance among you, and the alien, the orphan and the widow who are in your town, shall come and eat and be satisfied, in order that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work of your hand which you do.
v.NIV: so that the Levites (who have no allotment or inheritance of their own) and the foreigners, the fatherless and the widows who live in your towns may come and eat and be satisfied, and so that the Lord your God may bless you in all the work of your hands.
v.NRSV: the Levites, because they have no allotment or inheritance with you, as well as the resident aliens, the orphans, and the widows in your towns, may come and eat their fill so that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work that you undertake.
v.SAENEW: 당신들이 사는 성 안에, 유산도 없고 차지할 몫도 없는 레위 사람이나 떠돌이나 고아나 과부들이 와서 배불리 먹게 하십시오. 그러면 주 당신들의 하나님은 당신들이 경영하는 모든 일에 복을 내려 주실 것입니다."
v.WLB: 그리하여 너희 성에 사는 몫이나 기업이 없는 레위 사람들과 이방 사람들과 고아와 과부들이 와서 배불리 먹게 해 너희 하나님 여호와께서 너희 손으로 하는 모든 일에 복 주시도록 하라.”
